question: Consider the mixed-spin Blume-Capel model on the simple cubic lattice. The lattice is partitioned into two interpenetrating sublattices Lambda_1 and Lambda_2. Lambda_1 carries spin-1/2 sites (sigma_i = +/-1/2), Lambda_2 carries spin-1 sites (S_j in {-1, 0, +1}). The Hamiltonian is H = -J sum_{<ij>} sigma_i S_j - Delta sum_{j in Lambda_2} S_j^2, where J>0 is the ferromagnetic nearest-neighbor coupling (between Lambda_1 and Lambda_2 sites only) and Delta is the crystal-field anisotropy. As Delta increases at fixed J, the ferromagnetic-paramagnetic transition changes from second order to first order at the tricritical anisotropy Delta_t. Using the Wang-Landau Monte Carlo algorithm to determine the density of states, supplemented by Metropolis checks, locate the tricritical point and determine k_B T_t / J (quantity: kBTt_over_J). Output a JSON object {"kBTt_over_J": <value>}.

metadata.system: Mixed-spin (1/2,1) Blume-Capel model on simple cubic lattice, Wang-Landau + Metropolis MC
metadata.source_excerpt: In the mixed-spin Blume-Capel model with spin-1/2 and spin S (S=1,2,3) on the simple cubic lattice, the tricritical point is located at (Delta_t/J=2.978(1); k_B T_t/J=0.439(1)).
metadata.difficulty_note: Tricritical point; result is S-independent for S=1,2,3 on SC lattice; Wang-Landau density-of-states method
